natural_occurrence.description: A form of vitamin E particularly abundant in American diet due to high consumption of soybean and corn oils. Has unique antioxidant properties complementary to alpha-tocopherol.

manufacturing.description: Extracted primarily from soybean and corn oil deodorizer distillates. Gamma-tocopherol is the predominant tocopherol in these oil sources, making extraction economically efficient. Molecular formula: C28H48O2, Molecular weight: 416.7.

other_uses.pharmaceutical: Vitamin E supplements (often in mixed tocopherol formulations)

eu.conditions: Evaluated together with E306, E307, E309

usa.notes: Approved as antioxidant and nutrient supplement

regulatory_history.event: EFSA concludes no safety concern at reported uses and levels

adi_context.source_note: EFSA concluded no safety concern at reported uses and levels. Included in group ADI with other tocopherols. Essential nutrient form of vitamin E.

fun_facts.title: American Vitamin E
fun_facts.description: The most common form of vitamin E in the American diet due to widespread use of soybean and corn oils - Americans consume more gamma than alpha tocopherol!

fun_facts.title: Nitrogen Specialist
fun_facts.description: Uniquely effective at trapping reactive nitrogen species (like peroxynitrite), not just oxygen radicals - offers protection that alpha-tocopherol can't provide.

fun_facts.title: Soybean Treasure
fun_facts.description: Soybean oil is naturally high in gamma-tocopherol, and the deodorizer distillate from soy processing is a major commercial source.

fun_facts.title: Inflammation Fighter
fun_facts.description: Research suggests gamma-tocopherol may have superior anti-inflammatory properties compared to alpha-tocopherol, with unique benefits for cardiovascular health.

fun_facts.title: Different Antioxidant Strategy
fun_facts.description: While alpha-tocopherol is better at integrating into cell membranes, gamma-tocopherol excels at scavenging specific types of free radicals, making them complementary partners.
